success_surface: Sheet-Level Filtering and Repositioning with Structure Preservation
effective_behaviors: Explored spreadsheet structure programmatically before implementing final solution
Checked target sheet existence before creation or modification
Used values_only=True iteration to extract raw data for custom filtering logic
Implemented multi-condition OR filtering with proper index-based column access
Wrote headers and data at precisely specified positions (header row offset, data start row)
Cleared stale content beyond new data range to prevent residual data issues
Protected source file by saving to separate output path
Provided operational logging with filtered row counts and location details
evidence: Agent executed inspection code first (sheetnames, dimensions, all rows) before final solution
Used conditional check: if 'Sheet2' not in wb.sheetnames then create_sheet()
Iterated ws1.iter_rows(values_only=True) to get Python-native data for filtering
Applied OR logic: item == 'TELIVISION' or class_val == 'CLASS III' or class_val == 'CLASS IV'
Headers written at row 2, filtered data started at row 3 per instruction specification
Loop removed cells beyond max_row_needed to clean stale worksheet content
Loaded workbook from input path, saved to distinct output_path
Printed summary: number of filtered rows, header position, data start position

memory_items.title: Pre-Implementation Inspection
memory_items.lesson: Always explore the spreadsheet structure (sheet names, dimensions, sample rows) before writing the main manipulation code.
memory_items.generalization: Programmatic inspection prevents errors from incorrect assumptions about sheet availability, data types, or existing content.

memory_items.title: Target Sheet Existence Check
memory_items.lesson: Verify whether the target worksheet exists before attempting to access or write to it.
memory_items.generalization: Use wb.sheetnames to check presence; create the sheet only if missing to avoid KeyError exceptions.

memory_items.title: Raw Value Iteration for Filtering
memory_items.lesson: Extract cell values with values_only=True when implementing custom filtering or transformation logic.
memory_items.generalization: This provides native Python objects suitable for comparisons, string matching, and condition evaluation.

memory_items.title: Precise Row Offset Writing
memory_items.lesson: When instructions specify exact header and data start positions, use those offsets consistently during write operations.
memory_items.generalization: Header row and first data row indices should be explicitly calculated and applied, accounting for zero-based vs one-based indexing.

memory_items.title: Stale Content Cleanup
memory_items.lesson: After writing filtered results, clear any existing cells beyond the new content boundary.
memory_items.generalization: Residual data from previous operations can cause validation failures; nullify rows beyond max_row_needed.

memory_items.title: File Safety Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Load the source workbook for reading but always save the modified version to a different path.
memory_items.generalization: This preserves data integrity and allows rollback if the output fails validation.

memory_items.title: Operational Logging
memory_items.lesson: Report execution results with concrete metrics: number of rows processed, filtered, and written locations.
memory_items.generalization: Quantitative logging enables quick verification and debugging when automated tests run.

edits.content:
## 6. Advanced Filtering & Copy Operations

- **Inspection Before Action**: Run exploratory code to discover sheet names, dimensions, and sample row values before implementing filtering or extraction logic.
- **Target Sheet Safeguard**: Always check if the destination sheet exists (`if sheet_name not in wb.sheetnames`) before writing; create it if missing.
- **Raw Value Extraction**: For custom filtering with multiple conditions, iterate with `values_only=True` to get native Python objects instead of cell objects.
- **Index-Based Column Access**: Reference columns by list index (0-based) after extracting rows to enable complex OR/AND condition evaluation.
- **Offset-Aware Writing**: When headers and data have specified starting positions, calculate row offsets explicitly and apply consistently during the write loop.
- **Boundary Cleanup**: After writing results, iterate through rows beyond the new content boundary and set their values to None to remove stale data.
- **Output Metrics Logging**: Include filtered row count, target locations (e.g., "headers at X2, data starting at X3"), and total rows affected in printed output for verification.
